meta.notes: Fully rebalanced from consensus. All flows verified.

Anthropic.arrSource: Anthropic commentary 2026 — $30B ARR run-rate (single-source highest-quality figure parked separately per Decision D11)

colSubs: ChatGPT Plus, Claude Pro, Gemini Advanced
Direct API calls to OpenAI, Anthropic, Google
M365 Copilot, SF Agentforce, NOW Assist, Canva AI

cells.ex: ChatGPT Plus ($20/mo, ~11M subs), Claude Pro, Gemini Advanced
cells.note: Largest revenue pool but discretionary. Heavy free-tier subsidy. High churn risk.

cells.ex: Hobbyist devs, indie hackers, students using API keys
cells.note: Small but pure. Every cent = tokens. ~95% frontier, ~5% open models.

cells.ex: Perplexity Pro, Canva Free/Pro, Monica AI, Wrtn free tier
cells.note: Real usage. AI native absorbs API cost.

cells.ex: Canva AI features, Grammarly, Notion AI, Adobe Firefly
cells.note: AI bundled free into consumer SaaS tiers. Retention play, not revenue driver. Adobe Firefly: growth flat at 10-11%.
Growing. Usage varies widely across seats.

cells.ex: Startups on APIs, dev agencies, Vercel AI SDK users
cells.note: Pure demand. ~65% frontier, ~35% open (AI natives buying Together/Fireworks inference). Fastest growing.

cells.ex: Cursor Pro/Biz, Replit Teams, Jasper Biz, Intercom Fin
cells.note: Strong PMF. SMEs adopt AI natives faster than SaaS AI.

cells.ex: HubSpot Breeze, Zoho Zia, Freshworks Freddy, Canva Teams AI
cells.note: Feature checkbox on renewal, low activation. HubSpot Breeze: no separate revenue disclosed = immaterial.
Standalone AI subs — higher activation, users chose to buy it.

cells.ex: Morgan Stanley, Klarna, Stripe, JPMorgan, Razorpay, Infosys
cells.note: LARGEST FUTURE TAM. Small today but 100% real. ~95% frontier, ~5% open. Goldman: ->$50-80B by 2028.
Real usage — AI natives solving specific enterprise workflows.

cells.ex: M365 Copilot (list $5.4B, net ~$2.5-3.5B after 40-60% discounting), GitHub Copilot ($1-2.3B — the one that works), SF Agentforce ($800M claimed, ~$250-350M real), NOW Assist ($600M, ~$400-500M real — cleanest reporter)
cells.note: COGS test: SF inference est. $6-12M on $800M "AI rev" = 98% margin = not real AI. NOW: 92-97%. MSFT only one with real margin impact (cloud margin -3pp). Vendors claim ~$52B total, real incremental is ~$9-13B across whole market (3.5-5% of enterprise SaaS). 0 of 10 vendors show AI-driven growth acceleration.

enterpriseReality.name: M365 Copilot
enterpriseReality.arrSource: Microsoft FY26 Q3 earnings — 15M paid seats × $30/mo annualised = $5.4B list-billings
enterpriseReality.claimed: 15M seats x $30/mo = $5.4B list (verified, MSFT Q2 FY26 earnings)
enterpriseReality.usage: 35.8% active (Recon Analytics, 150K respondents). Only 5% of orgs past pilot (Gartner). 70% tried, only 8% kept choosing it.
enterpriseReality.real: ~$2.5-3.5B net (40-60% discounting per Citi/JPM). Cloud margin down 3pp (72->69%) — real AI infra cost.
enterpriseReality.flags: 3.3% of 450M installed base after 2 years. Market share FELL 18.8%->11.5% (Recon Analytics Jul->Jan). Auto-installing. Morgan Stanley: "wall of sorry."

arrClaimedVsReal.realNote: 40-60% effective discounting per Citi/JPM channel checks; cloud margin down 3pp = real AI infra cost.

enterpriseReality.archetype: Real workload, real cost, small share

_citations.bundling: Microsoft 365 Copilot pricing page — separate $30/user/mo add-on on top of E3/E5 SKUs (premium add-on, not absorbed).
_citations.cost: Microsoft FY26 Q3 earnings — Cloud gross margin down 3pp explicitly attributed to AI infrastructure costs.

enterpriseReality.watchSignals: 3.3% of 450M installed base after two years. Market share fell 18.8% → 11.5% (Recon Analytics). Auto-installing across tenants. Morgan Stanley described enterprise reception as a “wall of sorry.” Cloud margin down 3pp directly attributed to AI infra cost. Disclosure tier_1b but only ~1% of parent revenue.

enterpriseReality.name: GitHub Copilot
enterpriseReality.arrSource: MSFT earnings narrative — 4.7M paid subs, GitHub total $2B ARR; Copilot midpoint $1-2.3B → $1.65B
enterpriseReality.claimed: 4.7M paid subs (verified, MSFT earnings). GitHub total $2B ARR — Copilot est. $1-2.3B
enterpriseReality.usage: 42% market share of AI coding. 27-30% acceptance rate (not 55% — that was task speed). Lost $20/user/mo in 2023 (WSJ).
enterpriseReality.real: $1-2.3B — the ONE product where AI IS the product
enterpriseReality.flags: Genuinely works. But facing Cursor ($1B ARR), open-source alternatives. Microsoft added Anthropic models "at great expense to margins" (SemiAnalysis). Usage caps introduced mid-2025.
enterpriseReality.archetype: AI is the product, but small at parent scale

_citations.bundling: GitHub Copilot pricing page — three premium tiers ($10 Pro / $19 Business / $39 Enterprise) separate from base GitHub plans.
_citations.cost: SemiAnalysis Aug-2025 — Microsoft added Anthropic models 'at great expense to margins.' Usage caps introduced mid-2025 are explicit cost-control signal.

enterpriseReality.watchSignals: 42% market share of AI coding. Acceptance rate 27–30% (not the 55% headline — that was task speed). Microsoft added Anthropic models “at great expense to margins” (SemiAnalysis) — explicit AI cost attribution. Usage caps introduced mid-2025. Real product, real cost, real growth — but still <1% of MSFT total revenue, ~50–82% of GitHub-ARR base.

enterpriseReality.name: Google Workspace AI (Gemini in Workspace)
enterpriseReality.arrSource: Alphabet Q1 2026 commentary — Gemini-in-Workspace external AI seats; reconciles with compute_disclosures.json:googl_cloud_ai.copilot_excluded_2025_usd_b $1.5B (MS-declared analogue)
enterpriseReality.claimed: Gemini-in-Workspace AI add-on; bundled in Business/Enterprise tiers
enterpriseReality.usage: Strong attach in Workspace base; meaningful seat uplift.
enterpriseReality.flags: AI repackaging of existing Workspace SKU = trad_saas. Distinct from Gemini frontier (provider) and GCP (trad_compute) — see Decision D9.
enterpriseReality.archetype: Bundled at scale, immaterial at parent

_citations.bundling: Google Workspace pricing — Gemini repackaged into Business Standard/Plus and Enterprise tiers; no standalone AI add-on SKU after Jan-2025 consolidation.
_citations.cost: Alphabet Q1 2026 earnings call — no explicit AI-attributable margin compression in Cloud GM commentary; mostly capex-driven.

enterpriseReality.watchSignals: Strong attach in Workspace base; meaningful seat uplift. Repackaging of existing Workspace SKUs — no pricing model change. Distinct from Gemini frontier (provider) and GCP (trad_compute) per wq-097 D9. No explicit AI cost attribution in Alphabet GM commentary.

enterpriseReality.name: Salesforce Agentforce
enterpriseReality.arrSource: Salesforce Q4 FY26 earnings — Agentforce ACV component of $2.9B AI+Data line
enterpriseReality.claimed: $800M Agentforce ARR (within $2.9B "AI+Data" that includes Informatica acquisition)
enterpriseReality.usage: ~50% of 29K deals are free/seeded. COGS test: inference cost est. $6-12M/yr on $800M = 98%+ "AI margin"
enterpriseReality.real: ~$250-350M real GenAI (rest is Data Cloud, Informatica, legacy Einstein)
enterpriseReality.flags: $2.9B bundles Informatica ($1.5B acq.) + Data 360 + Agentforce. Gross margin flat at 77% = minimal AI COGS. AWU metric criticized as vanity metric. <2% of $41.5B revenue.

arrClaimedVsReal.realNote: Real GenAI ~$250-350M (rest is Data Cloud, Informatica, legacy Einstein); ~50% of 29K deals free/seeded.

enterpriseReality.archetype: Bold pricing, no real cost, no lift

_citations.bundling: Salesforce pricing — Agentforce metered at $2/conversation premium add-on, separate from Sales/Service Cloud SKUs.
_citations.cost: Salesforce Q4 FY26 earnings — non-GAAP gross margin flat at 77%; no observable AI-attributable compression despite Agentforce launch.

enterpriseReality.watchSignals: $2 / conversation pricing exists but isn’t exclusive — traditional Sales Cloud still the dominant commercial model. Gross margin flat at 77% = minimal AI COGS — high pricing posture without observable workload. ~50% of 29K deals are free or seeded. Agentforce represents under 2% of $41.5B revenue.

enterpriseReality.name: Databricks (Mosaic + AI)
enterpriseReality.arrSource: Databricks press 2025 — Mosaic AI + GenAI suite uplift on $3B+ run-rate; editorial AI-attributable share
enterpriseReality.claimed: Mosaic AI + DBRX + AI agents on Lakehouse; consumption + SKU mix
enterpriseReality.usage: Strong enterprise attach; AI workloads largest growth driver of 2025 ARR uplift.
enterpriseReality.flags: Private; reporting via press; AI-attributable share is editorial. Trad_saas because Lakehouse is the underlying product being AI-augmented.

_citations.bundling: Databricks pricing — Mosaic AI Model Serving + Vector Search + Agent Bricks metered as separate consumption SKUs on top of base Lakehouse compute.
_citations.cost: NA — Databricks is private; no public gross margin disclosure.

enterpriseReality.watchSignals: Strong enterprise attach. AI workloads called out as largest growth driver of 2025 ARR uplift. Already consumption-based — AI doesn’t change the model. Reporting via press, not disclosure. Trad_saas because Lakehouse is the underlying product being AI-augmented. GM not publicly available.

enterpriseReality.name: ServiceNow Now Assist
enterpriseReality.arrSource: ServiceNow Q4 2025 earnings — $600M+ Now Assist ACV
enterpriseReality.claimed: $600M+ Now Assist ACV (verified, Q4 2025 earnings)
enterpriseReality.usage: Pro Plus premium est. 30-60% (UpperEdge). COGS test: inference cost est. $15-50M/yr = 92-97% "AI margin"
enterpriseReality.real: ~$400-500M (cleanest reporter — AI is a genuine premium SKU)
enterpriseReality.flags: Most honest of the group. But Pro Plus bundles non-AI features. Gross margin down 1.7pp (79.2->77.5%) — slight AI cost impact. Hidden consumption-based "assists" fees.

arrClaimedVsReal.realNote: Cleanest reporter — AI is genuine premium SKU, but Pro Plus bundles non-AI features.

_citations.bundling: ServiceNow pricing — Pro Plus is a discrete premium SKU above Pro, marketed as the AI-bundled tier; Now Assist is the differentiator at the upgrade.
_citations.cost: ServiceNow Q4 2025 earnings — non-GAAP gross margin down 1.7pp (79.2% → 77.5%) with explicit AI / inference cost attribution in management commentary.

enterpriseReality.watchSignals: Pro Plus premium take est. 30–60% (UpperEdge) — but still a tier upgrade on the existing seat model, not a new pricing model. Inference cost est. $15–50M/yr on $600M+ ACV = 92–97% “AI margin.” Gross margin down 1.7pp (79.2 → 77.5%) with explicit AI attribution. Highest share-of-parent in the cohort and the cleanest disclosure trail.

enterpriseReality.name: Adobe Firefly
enterpriseReality.arrSource: Adobe FY25 earnings narrative — Firefly $400-500M, AI-first ARR $250M; midpoint $450M
enterpriseReality.claimed: Firefly ~$400-500M, AI-first ARR $250M
enterpriseReality.usage: 75% of Fortune 500 "adopted" but free tier inflates. Some cannibalizes stock photo revenue.
enterpriseReality.flags: Growth completely flat at 10-11% pre AND post AI. Firefly has moved the needle zero on top-line growth.

arrClaimedVsReal.realNote: Some cannibalises stock photo revenue; growth flat at 10-11% pre and post AI.

_citations.bundling: Adobe pricing — generative credits bundled into Creative Cloud tiers (no standalone Firefly subscription); Firefly Premium add-on exists but secondary.
_citations.cost: Adobe FY25 earnings — non-GAAP gross margin stable at ~88%; no AI-attributable compression in management commentary.

enterpriseReality.watchSignals: 75% of Fortune 500 “adopted” but free tier inflates. Generative credits represent a real pricing model layer (usage-tied) even though credits are bundled into Creative Cloud tiers. Growth completely flat at 10–11% pre AND post AI. GM stable at 88% — no observable AI cost in margins. Firefly has moved the needle zero on top-line growth.

enterpriseReality.name: Microsoft Dynamics 365 Copilot
enterpriseReality.arrSource: Microsoft FY26 Q3 commentary — Dynamics Copilot SKUs (Sales/Service/Finance/Supply Chain). Editorial estimate within $37B AI run-rate.
enterpriseReality.claimed: Dynamics 365 Copilot SKUs — Sales/Service/Finance/Supply Chain
enterpriseReality.usage: Smaller surface than M365 Copilot; targeted to Dynamics base
enterpriseReality.flags: Per Decision D9, sits inside Microsoft AI run-rate gross; carved out into trad_saas alongside M365 Copilot + GitHub Copilot.

_citations.bundling: Microsoft Dynamics 365 pricing — Sales/Service/Finance/Supply Chain Copilot SKUs $30–50/user/mo on top of base Dynamics seats (premium add-on).
_citations.cost: Microsoft FY26 Q3 earnings — Cloud GM compression (-3pp) is parent-level AI infra cost; Dynamics inherits the same shared GM by construction (Microsoft trio shares parent GM).

enterpriseReality.watchSignals: Smaller surface than M365 Copilot, targeted to Dynamics base. Per wq-097 D9, sits inside Microsoft AI run-rate gross. Inherits parent-level AI cost realisation (Microsoft cloud margin -3pp) — Microsoft trio shares parent GM, by design. Editorial estimate, immaterial at parent scale.

enterpriseReality.name: Notion AI
enterpriseReality.arrSource: Notion press commentary 2025 — $10/seat AI add-on; >$200M ARR-stack reported. Editorial uplift to $250M for early 2026.
enterpriseReality.claimed: $10/seat AI add-on; ~$200-300M ARR estimated late 2025
enterpriseReality.usage: Strong attach in mid-market; consumer take-rate softer.
enterpriseReality.flags: Private; reporting via press, not disclosure. Repackaging existing Notion product with AI = trad_saas.

_citations.bundling: Notion pricing — Notion AI is a $10/user/month add-on on top of Plus/Business/Enterprise seats (premium add-on, not absorbed).
_citations.cost: NA — Notion is private; no public gross margin disclosure.

enterpriseReality.watchSignals: Strong attach in mid-market; consumer take-rate softer. $10/seat AI add-on — premium tier on existing seat model. High share of own (private) ARR. Repackaging existing Notion product with AI. Reporting via press, not disclosure. GM not publicly available.

enterpriseReality.name: Intuit Assist
enterpriseReality.arrSource: Intuit FY26 narrative — Assist agentic AI rolling across QuickBooks/TurboTax/Mailchimp; editorial estimate of incremental seat uplift
enterpriseReality.claimed: Assist (agentic AI) bundled across QuickBooks/TurboTax/Mailchimp
enterpriseReality.usage: Active rollout 2025-26; usage growing
enterpriseReality.flags: Repackaging existing SKUs with AI = trad_saas.

_citations.bundling: Intuit pricing — Assist bundled into existing QuickBooks/TurboTax/Mailchimp tiers at no upcharge; no standalone AI SKU disclosed.
_citations.cost: Intuit FY26 narrative — non-GAAP gross margin ~83–84%, minimal AI-attributable movement; bundled inference cost not broken out.

enterpriseReality.watchSignals: Active rollout 2025–26; usage growing. Repackaging existing SKUs with AI — no pricing model change, no premium tier, no separate disclosure. Growth flat vs pre-AI baseline. No observable AI cost in Intuit GM.

enterpriseReality.name: Oracle Fusion AI
enterpriseReality.arrSource: Oracle Q3 FY26 commentary — Fusion AI bundled into SaaS apps; no discrete disclosure. Editorial estimate.
enterpriseReality.claimed: Fusion AI agents — bundled into Oracle SaaS apps; no discrete revenue disclosure
enterpriseReality.usage: Limited disclosure; small relative to OCI infra revenue
enterpriseReality.flags: Distinct from OCI (trad_compute). Per Decision D9, Oracle splits across Fusion AI (trad_saas) and OCI AI infra (trad_compute).

_citations.bundling: Oracle Fusion Cloud pricing — Fusion AI agents bundled into Cloud Apps SKUs without discrete AI add-on; no separately-priced AI tier.
_citations.cost: Oracle Q3 FY26 earnings — gross margin pressure visible at parent but driven by OCI capex/depreciation, not Fusion AI inference cost.

enterpriseReality.watchSignals: Distinct from OCI (trad_compute). Per wq-097 D9, Oracle splits across Fusion AI (trad_saas) and OCI AI infra (trad_compute). Limited public disclosure — bundled into Oracle SaaS without discrete revenue line. Margin pressure visible at parent but mostly OCI capex, not Fusion AI software cost.

enterpriseReality.name: Snowflake Cortex
enterpriseReality.arrSource: Snowflake Q4 FY26 commentary — Cortex AI suite; consumption-based; not separately disclosed. Editorial estimate.
enterpriseReality.claimed: Cortex AI/ML suite; consumption-based; not separately disclosed
enterpriseReality.usage: Bundled into Data Cloud consumption; meaningful but small share.
enterpriseReality.flags: AI-on-data feature stack; trad_saas because it's repackaging Snowflake data platform with AI.

_citations.bundling: Snowflake pricing — Cortex AI / ML functions metered as part of existing Data Cloud consumption credits; no separate AI SKU.
_citations.cost: Snowflake Q4 FY26 earnings — non-GAAP product gross margin stable; AI inference cost hidden inside total platform consumption with no discrete AI margin call-out.

enterpriseReality.watchSignals: Bundled into Data Cloud consumption — AI extends an already-consumption-based model. Material share of SNOW total revenue (~5%). AI-on-data feature stack repackages Snowflake’s existing platform. Margin stable; AI cost hidden inside total platform consumption.

enterpriseReality.name: Atlassian Intelligence / Rovo
enterpriseReality.arrSource: Atlassian FY26 Q3 commentary — Rovo paid SKU launched late 2025; editorial estimate from cloud uplift attribution
enterpriseReality.claimed: Rovo + Atlassian Intelligence — paid SKU launched late 2025; not separately disclosed
enterpriseReality.usage: Bundled with Premium/Enterprise; Rovo standalone available. Adoption modest.
enterpriseReality.flags: Atlassian growth narrative leans on AI but no discrete revenue line; reconcile against Cloud growth attribution.

enterpriseReality.name: SAP Joule
enterpriseReality.arrSource: SAP commentary — 'Joule bundled free in RISE/GROW; 2/3 of deals include AI'; no separate metering. Editorial midpoint of $0-200M range.
enterpriseReality.claimed: "2/3 of deals include AI" — Joule bundled free in RISE/GROW
enterpriseReality.usage: No separate metering. No AI revenue disclosed = immaterial.
enterpriseReality.real: ~$0-200M. Like saying "100% of iPhones include Siri"
enterpriseReality.flags: Cloud growth (23%) driven by S/4HANA forced migration (ECC end-of-life 2027), NOT AI. No analyst attributes growth to Joule.

arrClaimedVsReal.realNote: Like saying '100% of iPhones include Siri.' Cloud growth (23%) driven by S/4HANA forced migration, not Joule.

enterpriseReality.name: Zendesk AI
enterpriseReality.arrSource: Zendesk press commentary 2025 — Advanced AI add-on $50/agent/mo; editorial estimate
enterpriseReality.claimed: Advanced AI add-on at $50/agent/mo + Resolution Bot consumption
enterpriseReality.usage: Mid-market attach growing; enterprise slower
enterpriseReality.flags: Private (Permira-owned); disclosure thin.

enterpriseReality.name: HubSpot Breeze
enterpriseReality.arrSource: HubSpot FY25 earnings narrative — Breeze AI bundled into Hubs; no discrete disclosure. Editorial.
enterpriseReality.claimed: Breeze AI agents/copilot bundled into Hubs
enterpriseReality.usage: Bundled — no AI metering
enterpriseReality.flags: Breeze bundled into existing Hub SKUs; not a paid add-on.

enterpriseReality.name: Box AI
enterpriseReality.arrSource: Box FY26 narrative — Enterprise Plus AI tier; small contribution. Editorial.
enterpriseReality.claimed: Box AI in Enterprise Plus tier; consumption-based
enterpriseReality.usage: Small share of revenue mix
enterpriseReality.flags: Small surface area relative to other trad_saas entries.

enterpriseReality.name: GitHub Advanced Security (AI features)
enterpriseReality.arrSource: GitHub commentary — AI-augmented security scanning bundled with Advanced Security SKU
enterpriseReality.claimed: AI features within GitHub Advanced Security
enterpriseReality.usage: Bundled — small AI attribution
enterpriseReality.flags: Small surface area; included for completeness within the MSFT trad_saas family.

enterpriseReality.name: Workday AI
enterpriseReality.arrSource: Workday narrative — 'growing demand for AI SKUs'; no AI metric disclosed. Editorial midpoint of $0-100M range.
enterpriseReality.claimed: Not disclosed — "growing demand for AI SKUs"
enterpriseReality.usage: No AI metric = immaterial. Flex Credits model.
enterpriseReality.flags: Growth crashing: 21%->13%. AI has zero visible impact.

arrClaimedVsReal.realNote: Growth crashing 21%->13%; AI has zero visible impact.

enterpriseReality.name: Zoom AI Companion
enterpriseReality.arrSource: Zoom FY26 narrative — AI Companion bundled free in paid plans; no discrete revenue. Editorial.
enterpriseReality.claimed: AI Companion bundled in paid Zoom plans (no incremental fee)
enterpriseReality.usage: High activation but immaterial revenue impact (no premium SKU)
enterpriseReality.flags: Bundled-free model means revenue impact is the seat-attach lift, not a discrete AI line.

topConsumers.ev: Live dashboard: $1.3M/day spend, $475M/yr managed. 35%/wk growth. Gateway passthrough (not additive to provider totals).
OpenRouter #1 app (829B tokens/day, 15.8M requests/day). Personal agent platform launched Jan 2026. Fastest-growing consumer on OpenRouter.
topConsumers.arrSource: OpenClaw press 2026 — recent launch; ~$10M editorial

topConsumers.ev: $1B+ ARR, 40-60% COGS ratio. Revenue/price: 1-1.6T. Bottom-up: 400-800B. COGS: 680-980B. Midpoint ~800B.
topConsumers.arrSource: Pre-existing entry; provenance backfilled to Tier 2B as part of wq-096

topConsumers.ev: Self-reported 8.4T/mo. Most reliable figure — transparent platform with public pricing. Gateway passthrough.
topConsumers.arrSource: Pre-existing entry; provenance backfilled to Tier 2B as part of wq-096

topConsumers.ev: OpenRouter #2 app (271B/day); VS Code 904K installs. CLI agent + IDE extension for autonomous coding.
topConsumers.arrSource: Kilo Code press 2025 — small commercial ARR; ~$8M editorial

topConsumers.ev: 780M queries/mo, $200M ARR. Revenue/price: ~220B. Bottom-up: 91-130B (but multi-step pipelines inflate). COGS: 220-290B.
topConsumers.arrSource: entities.json (apply_pipeline)

topConsumers.ev: $240-265M rev but only ~60% AI-related. 50M registered != DAU (likely <2M). Revenue/price: 260-310B. COGS: ~192B. Bottom-up much lower.
topConsumers.arrSource: Pre-existing entry; provenance backfilled to Tier 2B as part of wq-096

topConsumers.ev: $155M ARR combined. Agent sessions burn 50-200K tokens each. Revenue/price: 178-212B. COGS: ~148B.
topConsumers.arrSource: Pre-existing entry; provenance backfilled to Tier 2B as part of wq-096

topConsumers.ev: 47.7M DAU but most exercises pre-generated. $10-30M/yr AI spend (margin compression data). At $0.30-0.50/M: 110-183B.
topConsumers.arrPendingReason: Duolingo Max AI-tier disclosed but not as discrete ARR line

topConsumers.ev: Lean AI Leaderboard. $400M ARR, 40 employees. Triangulated: COGS method ($8-12M/mo API spend at $2-3/M = 3-5T/mo = 100-170B/day), user method (100K DAU × 10-15 prompts × 50K tokens = 60-90B/day). Central estimate ~100B/day.
topConsumers.arrSource: Pre-existing entry; provenance backfilled to Tier 2B as part of wq-096

topConsumers.ev: 800M AI uses/mo but most are tiny interactions (~1K tokens). "Trillion Token Club" = cumulative, not daily. COGS: 85-170B.
topConsumers.arrPendingReason: Canva is a digital-native platform — AI-attributable ARR not separately disclosed; would require split from total Canva ARR ($3B+)

topConsumers.ev: OpenRouter #4 (69B/day); VS Code 3.5M installs. Open-source autonomous coding agent.

topConsumers.ev: Acquired by Google for talent. 20M+ MAU. Own models, high volume per session (roleplay = long context).

topConsumers.ev:
Fathom is an AI-powered meeting assistant that automatically records, transcribes, highlights, and summarizes video calls on platforms like Zoom, Google Meet, and Microsoft Teams.

Sources: Crunchbase funding data, TechCrunch coverage, ProductHunt launch, LinkedIn employee count, general SaaS freemium benchmarking
Confidence: low — Fathom is a private company with limited public financial disclosures. They raised a ~$17-18M seed/Series A round (reported ~2022-2023), have a freemium model with a paid tier (Fathom Team Edition), and have reported strong user growth (millions of users). ARR estimate is a rough extrapolation based on funding size, team size, and freemium conversion typical for this category — no verified press report of exact ARR figure.

events.title: The GPT-4 Era Begins
events.body: OpenAI dominates with GPT-4 at $3.4B ARR, processing ~5T tokens/day via API. Google quietly runs more total tokens through Search and Workspace but generates a fraction of the revenue. Anthropic is a $200M niche player. China's AI market is nascent — ByteDance hasn't launched Doubao yet. Total market: ~18T tokens/day, almost entirely US-driven.

events.title: GPT-4o + Claude 3.5 + Llama 3 Launch
events.body: Three inflection points in one quarter. OpenAI ships GPT-4o (multimodal, faster, cheaper). Anthropic releases Claude 3.5 Sonnet, winning developer mindshare. Meta open-sources Llama 3, igniting the open-model movement. ByteDance launches Doubao at 120B tokens/day — tiny, but the start of a 1,000x growth curve. Token volumes double as prices halve.

events.title: The Mini Model Revolution
events.body: Small, cheap models change the economics. GPT-4o mini, Claude 3.5 Haiku, and Gemini Flash slash inference costs by 10-50x. API pricing collapses from $20/M to under $1/M for capable models. This unlocks massive developer adoption — every startup can now afford AI. Enterprise pilots proliferate but actual usage remains shallow.

events.title: Open Source Inflection Point
events.body: Llama 3.1 405B proves open models can match frontier performance. DeepSeek V2 emerges with shockingly good performance at a fraction of the cost. Chinese models hit 61% of OpenRouter token volume. The open-vs-closed debate shifts from 'if' to 'when'. ByteDance hits 2T tokens/day. OpenAI crosses $7B ARR but inference costs exceed revenue.

events.title: The DeepSeek Shock
events.body: DeepSeek V3 trains for $6M (vs GPT-4's $79M) and matches frontier performance. The AI cost narrative inverts — it's no longer about who spends the most, but who spends the smartest. China's token consumption explodes. ByteDance reaches 8T/day. Anthropic hits $4B ARR with 70% from API, proving the enterprise AI market is real. OpenAI races to $20B ARR.

events.title: The Enterprise AI Reality Check
events.body: Microsoft Copilot has 15M paid seats but only 35.8% actually use it. Gartner finds only 5% of enterprises are past pilot stage. Salesforce gives away 50% of Agentforce deals for free. The gap between AI hype and enterprise reality becomes undeniable. Meanwhile, developer and API usage grows 20x — the real demand is bottom-up, not top-down.

events.title: Anthropic Takes Enterprise, China Takes Volume
events.body: Anthropic becomes the enterprise inference leader (Barclays), hitting $14B ARR in February. Claude Code launches at >$2.5B ARR. But the volume story is in China — ByteDance passes 45T tokens/day, overtaking Google as the world's largest token processor. Alibaba's internal AI usage hits 16T/day. China's National Data Administration reports 140T+ tokens/day nationally.

events.title: The AI-Native Explosion
events.body: Cursor, Perplexity, and a wave of AI-native apps cross $100M+ ARR. Coding becomes the dominant use case — over 50% of OpenRouter traffic is now programming (up from 11%). OpenAI launches Codex with 70% month-over-month growth. The token mix shifts from chat to agents and multi-step workflows, multiplying tokens per task by 10-100x.

events.title: Revenue Explosion Meets the China Surge
events.body: OpenAI hits $2B/month revenue and 900M weekly active users. Anthropic reaches $19B ARR. But the biggest story is ByteDance at 110T tokens/day — more than all Western providers combined. China represents ~48% of global tokens. Total market: ~320T tokens/day across 14+ major providers. The industry generates $52B ARR but frontier providers still operate at a loss — spending $2.25-$2.60 for every $1 of revenue.

entries.note: Supply-side check — is build-out coherent with revenue path

cumulative._doc: Cumulative 2023–2025 totals. Buyer-gross is the public headline number (what customers actually paid; includes channel margins). Net is provider-received chPass. Years without a market_aggregates entry fall back to per-entity collected_revenue summing where available. Tokens require an annual aggregation field on market_aggregates that doesn't yet exist for prior years; falls back to null and the front-end keeps its hardcoded ~360T display.

arr_external_estimate.methodology_note: Anthropic calculates annualised revenue as last 4 weeks of API revenue × 13 + monthly chatbot subscription revenue (single-day active subs anchor) × 12 (per The Information). Selecting a single-day anchor and 4-week-window can inflate vs steady-state. Published number on TAIL = $30B (Anthropic-disclosed); $44B carried as external estimate only.

current.tokens_per_day_methodology: Editorial estimate: ~4.7M paid seats (MSFT Q4 FY25 earnings) × ~50–70% active × ~50–200 requests/day × ~1–5K tokens/req → ~0.5–1.5 T/day; central 1.0 T/day. Cross-check: GitHub Copilot reported as 2nd largest customer of Anthropic models (Zitron 2026-05-06). Anthropic total ~6 T/day → if GitHub is ~10–20% of Anthropic volume that's 0.6–1.2 T/day from Anthropic alone, plus residual OpenAI portion. Replaces unsourced 1000.0 T/day legacy value — wq-094 cleanup.
current.paid_seats_source: MSFT Q4 FY25 earnings (cited assumptions-audit.md row 12)
current.compute_subsidy_note: Microsoft subsidises customer compute spend on GitHub Copilot — published ARR overstates economic margin. June 2026 token-based billing transition will alter both revenue trajectory and margin disclosure. Watch Q3 2026 earnings.

layer_stack_ratios.apps_revenue_2025_source: Live read from site-data.json:sankey.totalCustomerRevenue (~$17.36B as of 2026-05-06) — the Revenue Ledger cohort total (Tier 1A-derived). NOT editorially multiplied to ecosystem scale; the cohort-vs-ecosystem gap is flagged on /methodology.html as a future-Apps-Ledger scope.
layer_stack_ratios.silicon_revenue_2025_source: NVIDIA Data Center revenue FY26 (calendar 2025) ~$135B + AMD MI300/MI350 ~$5B + custom ASIC (Broadcom, Marvell) ~$25B
layer_stack_ratios.power_revenue_2025_source: Editorial — DC power + utilities AI-attributable. Power Ledger placeholder (wq-006).
layer_stack_ratios._layer_stack_gross_doc: Layer-stack ratios anchored on GROSS apps revenue (site-data.sankey.totalCustomerRevenue_gross). The homepage hero stack reads against gross because the Revenue pill shows gross. Existing apps_to_compute_2025 etc. anchor on NET — preserved for backwards compat with prior consumers.

msft_ai.label: Microsoft Azure AI
msft_ai.segment_basis: MSFT 2025 calendar uses sum-of-quarterlies basis ($25.25B) per wq-092 D1, NOT annualised run-rate basis ($28B Microsoft self-disclosed; preserved as value_2025_annualised_run_rate_basis_usd_b for context). The two diverge because MSFT AI grew rapidly through 2025 (+123% YoY into Q1 26): sum-of-Q < annualised when growth accelerates. Trajectory + Layer Stack lookback use sum-of-Q so quarterly chart shows monotonic growth Q1 25 → Q1 26 (no Q-o-Q drops). Segment shares (proportional to annualised $28B): Frontier lab compute 64.3% → $16.23B sum-of-Q (OpenAI ~80% Azure-resident); Hosted model APIs gross 5.4% → $1.35B (Azure OpenAI Service to enterprise; Pass-through 20% per Zitron-leaked rev-share); AI workload compute 3.9% → $0.99B; Copilot excluded 26.4% → $6.67B (MS-declared scope-out: M365 Copilot list billings + GitHub Copilot + Copilot Studio/Sales/Service). Tie-out (sum-of-Q): 16.23+0.99+1.35+6.67 = $25.24B ≈ $25.25B ✓. Q1 26 quarterly = $9.25B (= $37B disclosed run-rate ÷ 4 per Q1 26 earnings).

ai_run_rate_disclosure.source_quote: Microsoft's AI business surpassed an annual revenue run rate of $37 billion, up 123% year-over-year.
ai_run_rate_disclosure.source: Microsoft FY26 Q3 earnings press release / call commentary

msft_ai.notes: Segment-decomposed per wq-091; calendar basis switched to sum-of-quarterlies under wq-092 D1 (was annualised run-rate basis under wq-091, which produced Q4 25 ≥ Q1 26 trajectory). Sum-of-Q $25.25B ($28B annualised retained as context field). Frontier lab compute dominates — OpenAI's compute spend on Azure is the central circular-financing line on the page. Copilot scope-out is MS-declared per wq-091 D4. See dec-2026-05-06-compute-ledger-bucket-sizing.md §Issue 3 for full Copilot derivation.

googl_cloud_ai.label: Google Cloud AI
googl_cloud_ai.segment_basis: GCP 2025 calendar = $7B both sum-of-quarterlies AND annualised bases (bottom-up build does not diverge between bases). Per wq-091, $7B replaces wq-089's $18B editorial 30%-of-cloud overstatement; per wq-092 D4, Q1 26 raised from $2.50B (wq-091) to $3.00B — re-derived from '+800% YoY on gen AI products' narrative back-cast (Tier 2A editorial), ensures growth from Q4 25 ($2.80B) into Q1 26 (+7%). Quarterly trajectory per Final Locked Trajectory: Q1 25 $0.70B → Q2 $1.40B → Q3 $2.10B → Q4 $2.80B → Q1 26 $3.00B (sum 25 = $7.00B; monotonic Q-o-Q growth across the chart). Frontier lab compute ($3.5B 2025): Anthropic on TPUs $1.5–2B (deep partnership, April 2026 expanded multi-GW agreement from 2027); OpenAI on GCP $0.5–1B (CNBC 2025-07-16 partial-year diversification); Mistral + Cohere + AI21 + others ~$1B. Per D5 wq-091, internal-recursion (Google's own Gemini compute on GCP) contributes $0. Hosted model APIs ($0.5B): Vertex partner-served. AI workload compute ($1.5B). Copilot excluded $1.5B (MS-declared analogue): Gemini-in-Workspace external Apps revenue. The $36.9B Q1 2026 Anthropic equity gain is investment income, NOT Cloud operating revenue (Fortune 2026-04-30) — does not inflate this line. Tie-out: 3.5+1.5+0.5+1.5 = $7.0B ✓.

ai_run_rate_disclosure.narrative_quote: revenue from products built on Google's gen AI models grew nearly 800% year-over-year
ai_run_rate_disclosure.thirteen_product_lines_quote: 13 product lines now exceed $1B annual run rate
ai_run_rate_disclosure.source: Alphabet Q1 2026 earnings call commentary; AI portion of Cloud is bottom-up Tier 2A estimate (no discrete GCP AI revenue figure disclosed).

googl_cloud_ai.notes: Restated downward from $18B (wq-089) to $7B (wq-091) per bottom-up build vs prior 30% editorial AI-share weight on GCP. Per wq-092 D4, Q1 26 raised from $2.50B to $3.00B to ensure monotonic Q4 25 → Q1 26 growth on the trajectory chart (was a ~32% Q-o-Q drop under wq-091). The $0.50B uplift is re-derived from the +800% YoY gen-AI-products narrative — under wq-091 Q1 26 was set to $2.50B as a normalisation-from-Q4-spike heuristic; under wq-092 we treat the late-Q4-2025 Anthropic-on-TPUs + OpenAI-on-GCP ramps as continuing into Q1 26 rather than dipping. Per D5 wq-091, internal-recursion (Google's own Gemini compute) does NOT contribute to Frontier lab compute. See dec-2026-05-06-compute-ledger-bucket-sizing.md §Issues 5 + 6.

amzn_aws_ai.label: AWS AI
amzn_aws_ai.segment_basis: AWS 2025 calendar = $10B both sum-of-quarterlies AND annualised bases (Jassy's $15B Q1 2026 run-rate × 4 implies $15B annualised exit but sum-of-Q for 2025 calendar lands at the same $10B). Quarterly trajectory restated per wq-092 Final Locked Trajectory to ensure monotonic growth into Q1 26 (under wq-091, Q3=Q4=Q1 26=$3.75B was a flat plateau; under wq-092, Q4 25 = $3.60B → Q1 26 = $3.75B = +4% Q-o-Q). Trajectory: Q1 25 $1.50B → Q2 $2.20B → Q3 $2.70B → Q4 $3.60B → Q1 26 $3.75B (Jassy ÷ 4). Frontier lab compute ($7.0B 2025): Anthropic 2025 revenue $4.71B with compute spend exceeding revenue — Anthropic on AWS infra ~60% (training + inference) ~$5–6B + minor labs (Mistral via Bedrock partner, Cohere, AI21, smaller labs) ~$1–2B. Hosted model APIs ($2.5B 2025): Bedrock customer-paid resale of frontier models. Pass-through 25% blended. AI workload compute ($0.5B): non-frontier residual. Copilot excluded $0: no AWS Copilot-equivalent. Tie-out: 7.0+0.5+2.5+0 = $10.0B ✓. CoreWeave double-count note: ~$3.8B of CoreWeave's $4.5B AI line is Microsoft-sub-rent for OpenAI compute — captured separately under msft_ai Frontier lab compute and under coreweave Frontier lab compute (same flow, different legs of ASC 606 principal treatment).

ai_run_rate_disclosure.narrative_quote: AWS's AI business is generating a $15 billion annualized revenue run rate.
ai_run_rate_disclosure.ceo_anchor_quote: Andy Jassy 2026 shareholder letter: AWS AI revenue crossed a $15B annualized run rate.
ai_run_rate_disclosure.source: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy 2026 shareholder letter (early 2026); plus Q1 2026 earnings call narrative — Bedrock client spending +170% Q-over-Q.

amzn_aws_ai.notes: Restated downward from $18B (wq-089) to $10B (wq-091) per Andy Jassy CEO disclosure of $15B Q1 2026 run-rate. Quarterly trajectory restated under wq-092 to eliminate the wq-091 Q3=Q4=Q1 26 flat plateau (Q4 25 now $3.60B → Q1 26 $3.75B = +4% Q-o-Q growth, modest but monotonic). Tier annotations on Frontier lab compute / Hosted model APIs are 1B (CEO disclosure anchor). AWS-OpenAI Q1 2026 announcement of $38B + $100B/8yr expansion is RPO not realised revenue and does not affect 2025 segments. See dec-2026-05-06-compute-ledger-bucket-sizing.md §Issue 4.

oracle_cloud_ai.label: Oracle OCI AI
oracle_cloud_ai.segment_basis: OCI 2025 calendar (sum-of-quarterlies basis) = $2.95B (annualised exit run-rate = $3B; preserved as context). Frontier lab compute ($1.97B 2025): OpenAI Stargate workload billing through OCI as data centers come online late 2025; partial-year contribution. The contracted $300B+ OpenAI commitment is RPO not realised revenue. AI workload compute ($0.98B): general OCI AI workload. No Hosted model APIs (Oracle has no Bedrock/AOAI/Vertex equivalent). Copilot excluded $0. Tie-out: 1.97+0.98+0+0 = $2.95B ✓. Update each cycle as Stargate billings ramp.

ai_run_rate_disclosure.narrative_quote: AI-attributable portion of OCI; Oracle does not separately disclose AI revenue. OCI growth narrative driven by 'unprecedented AI demand' and RPO disclosure of $300B+ OpenAI/Stargate commitment.
ai_run_rate_disclosure.source: Oracle Q3 FY26 earnings call commentary; AI-attributable line is editorial 2A estimate.

underlying_segment.note: Cloud revenue includes both OCI and SaaS (Fusion + NetSuite). OCI is the AI-relevant subset; AI portion of OCI is editorial.

oracle_cloud_ai.notes: ORCL fiscal year ends May; Q3 FY26 = Dec 2025 – Feb 2026 quarter. Tighten as Stargate revenue ramps through 2026. AI line $3B holds from wq-089; per-segment reallocation moves Stargate portion to Frontier lab compute (was AI workload compute under wq-089).

coreweave.label: CoreWeave
coreweave.segment_basis: CoreWeave 2025 calendar (sum-of-Q basis) = $4.23B (annualised exit run-rate $4.5B preserved as context — 10-Q disclosed). Frontier lab compute ($3.76B 2025): ~85% of CoreWeave revenue is Microsoft sub-rent — much of which is OpenAI workload Microsoft sub-rents to CoreWeave to handle peak frontier-lab demand. The 'circular financing made visible' line on the Layer Stack. Note — at OpenAI-line resolution this is the same flow as msft_ai Frontier lab compute; ASC 606 principal treatment on each entity's P&L means both are real revenue lines that aggregate gross-of-double-count by definition. AI workload compute ($0.47B): other AI Native + research customers on CoreWeave directly. No Hosted model APIs — pure-play GPU cloud. Tie-out (sum-of-Q): 3.76+0.47+0+0 = $4.23B ✓.
coreweave.notes: CoreWeave IPO'd March 2025. The Microsoft-sub-rent-for-OpenAI line is editorially the most important callout for the Layer Stack — it's where the circular-financing geometry is most visible to a non-specialist reader. Per-segment reallocation under wq-091: Frontier lab compute up $0.2B / AI workload compute down $0.2B vs wq-089 — Microsoft-sub-rent share of total revenue is more concentrated on frontier-lab work than the prior 84%/16% split implied.

nebius.label: Nebius
nebius.segment_basis: Nebius 2025 calendar (sum-of-Q basis) = $0.89B (annualised $0.95B preserved). Frontier lab compute ($0.61B 2025): Mistral and other smaller frontier labs renting capacity from Nebius (US-Europe footprint). AI workload compute ($0.28B): AI Native customers + research. No Hosted model APIs. Tie-out (sum-of-Q): 0.61+0.28+0+0 = $0.89B ✓.
nebius.notes: Nebius (NBIS) — formerly Yandex N.V. assets, US-listed AI cloud. 6-K filer. Per-segment split is editorial 2A estimate; tighten if Nebius discloses customer concentration. Per-segment reallocation under wq-091: Frontier lab compute slightly higher / AI workload compute slightly lower vs wq-089 to align with neoclouds aggregate per Final Locked Table.

lambda.label: Lambda Labs
lambda.segment_basis: Lambda 2025 calendar (sum-of-Q basis) = $0.56B (annualised $0.60B preserved). Frontier lab compute ($0.47B 2025): smaller frontier labs + research orgs renting GPU capacity. AI workload compute ($0.09B): AI Native customers + sole-trader / academic. No Hosted model APIs. Tie-out (sum-of-Q): 0.47+0.09+0+0 = $0.56B ✓.
lambda.notes: Private. Press-disclosed run rate; tighten if Lambda files S-1. Per-segment split is 2A editorial.

crusoe.label: Crusoe
crusoe.segment_basis: Crusoe 2025 calendar (sum-of-Q basis) = $0.37B (annualised $0.40B preserved). Frontier lab compute ($0.28B 2025): smaller labs + applied-research customers renting GPU capacity. AI workload compute ($0.09B): AI Native + general research. No Hosted model APIs. Tie-out (sum-of-Q): 0.28+0.09+0+0 = $0.37B ✓.
crusoe.notes: Private. Press-disclosed. Per-segment split is 2A editorial.

compute._d6_gate: PASS — principal-everywhere confirmed per data/methodology_constants.json:compute_revenue.principal_agent_treatment; Copilot scope-out applied; pass-through scoped to Hosted model APIs only.
compute._notes: Three-segment model: Frontier lab compute (the circular-financing line — Model Providers paying external hyperscalers/neoclouds), AI workload compute (AI Natives + enterprises + sovereign workloads), Hosted model APIs (packaged token APIs). Pass-through applies to Hosted model APIs only. Copilot scoped OUT — tracked separately under copilot_excluded_*. See /methodology.html §Compute Revenue — three-segment model.

computeProviders._doc: wq-096 — Tier 4 (trad_compute) and Tier 5 (ai_native_compute) records. tradCompute carries appsCarveout pointing at trad_saas record IDs whose ARR is deducted to produce arrNetOfAppsCarveout. Per Decision D9, multi-tier companies (Google, Microsoft, Oracle) split into separate records per tier; Microsoft has no Frontier entry.

tradCompute.name: AWS AI
tradCompute.arrSource: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy 2026 shareholder letter — '$15B annualised run-rate'
tradCompute.appsCarveoutNote: AWS has no Copilot-equivalent productivity SaaS. Q-Developer and Bedrock pass-through are infra/compute, not Apps. Carve-out is empty.

tradCompute.name: Microsoft Azure AI
tradCompute.arrSource: Microsoft FY26 Q3 earnings — '$37B annualised AI run-rate, +123% YoY'
tradCompute.appsCarveoutNote: Decision D9: Microsoft has NO frontier entry; Azure carves out M365 Copilot + GitHub Copilot + Dynamics Copilot which are trad_saas. Net = gross − sum(arrClaimedNumeric of carveout records).

tradCompute.name: Google Cloud AI
tradCompute.arrSource: Editorial Tier 2A — bottom-up from GCP Q1 2026 AI revenue $3.0B × 4 (per data/compute_disclosures.json:googl_cloud_ai.frontier_lab_compute_q1_2026 + ai_workload_compute + hosted_model_apis_gross + copilot_excluded), grossed for Workspace AI
tradCompute.appsCarveoutNote: Decision D9: Google splits across Gemini (frontier), Workspace AI (trad_saas), GCP ex-Workspace (trad_compute). Carve out Workspace AI from gross.

tradCompute.name: Oracle OCI AI
tradCompute.arrSource: Oracle Q3 FY26 earnings narrative — OCI AI portion editorial Tier 2A; Q1 26 quarterly $0.9B × 4
tradCompute.appsCarveoutNote: Decision D9: Oracle splits across Fusion AI (trad_saas) and OCI AI infra (trad_compute). Carve out Fusion AI.

aiNativeCompute.name: Anyscale
aiNativeCompute.arrSource: Anyscale press — ~$50M ARR estimated 2025; Ray-platform based AI serving

aiNativeCompute.name: CoreWeave
aiNativeCompute.arrSource: CoreWeave Q1 2026 10-Q — annualised exit run-rate (Q1 26 quarterly $1.5B × 4 = $6.0B)
aiNativeCompute.doubleCountNote: ~85% of CoreWeave revenue is Microsoft sub-rent for OpenAI workload — captured in passthrough_aiNativeCompute_to_tradCompute layer.

aiNativeCompute.name: Crusoe
aiNativeCompute.arrSource: Crusoe press-disclosed — annualised exit run-rate (Q1 26 quarterly ~$0.12B × 4)

aiNativeCompute.name: Fal.ai
aiNativeCompute.arrSource: Fal.ai press 2025 — ~$95M ARR end-2025; generative-media model serving

aiNativeCompute.name: Fireworks AI
aiNativeCompute.arrSource: Fireworks press 2025 — ~$100M ARR late 2025; editorial uplift to $140M for early 2026

aiNativeCompute.name: Groq
aiNativeCompute.arrSource: Groq press 2025 — Bain estimates $90M ARR late 2025

aiNativeCompute.name: Lambda Labs
aiNativeCompute.arrSource: Lambda press-disclosed — annualised exit run-rate (Q1 26 quarterly ~$0.18B × 4)

aiNativeCompute.name: Nebius
aiNativeCompute.arrSource: Nebius (NBIS) Q1 2026 6-K — annualised exit run-rate (Q1 26 quarterly $0.34B × 4)

aiNativeCompute.name: Together AI
aiNativeCompute.arrSource: Together AI press 2025 — ~$100M ARR mid-2025; editorial uplift to $130M

arrModel._doc: Five-tier revenue model aggregator. apps_total + compute_net = industry_total within $0.1B tolerance. ai_native_total / trad_total cross-cut the apps/compute split per the editorial axis (Decision D1) and do not sum to industry_total because pass-through deductions are netted at the compute layer.

entries.valueBasis: Q1 2026 annualised — Anthropic's AWS compute spend
entries.evidence: Anthropic + Amazon disclosures 2025-26; ~60% of Anthropic compute on AWS; aggregate compute spend > Anthropic's $30B ARR
entries.rationale: Anthropic's compute spend exceeds its revenue (training-heavy phase). AWS share ~60% of compute.

entries.valueBasis: Q1 2026 annualised — Anthropic's GCP/TPU compute spend
entries.evidence: The Information 2026-04-30 — Anthropic-Google $200B 5-year commitment; Q1 2026 annualised flow ~$1.8B (early in ramp)
entries.rationale: Deep Anthropic-on-TPUs partnership; April 2026 expanded multi-GW agreement from 2027. Q1 26 actual flow modest pre-ramp.

entries.valueBasis: Q1 2026 annualised — OpenAI AWS workloads (post Q1 2026 announcement)
entries.evidence: AWS-OpenAI Q1 2026 announcement of $38B + $100B/8yr expansion is RPO; realised early flow modest
entries.rationale: Recently announced; realised revenue Q1 26 small relative to RPO.

entries.valueBasis: Q1 2026 annualised — OpenAI's Azure compute spend
entries.evidence: Bloomberg/Information 2025-26 reporting; Zitron 2026-05-06 leak corroborates Azure-resident OpenAI inference at $18.5B annualised exit run-rate (compute-paid back to Microsoft est. ~$6.5B share of OpenAI's $25B ARR)
entries.rationale: OpenAI's compute spend on Azure dwarfs all other frontier-to-trad flows. Estimated as ~26% of OpenAI's $25B ARR run-rate.

entries.valueBasis: Q1 2026 annualised — OpenAI Stargate billings via OCI
entries.evidence: Oracle Q3 FY26 RPO disclosure mentions $300B+ OpenAI commitment; realised flow Q1 26 partial-year as DCs come online (Q1 26 quarterly $0.6B × 4)
entries.rationale: Stargate compute billing through OCI; partial-year contribution.

longTailMultiplier.baseValueBasis: Aggregate ARR of frontier labs ex-OpenAI/Anthropic (xAI, Mistral, Cohere, DeepSeek, Alibaba/Qwen, Baidu, Tencent, Minimax, Moonshot, ByteDance, Meta) — ~$3.5B 2026-Q1 annualised
longTailMultiplier.rationale: Long-tail frontier labs spend ~25% of ARR on trad-compute (lower than OpenAI/Anthropic because many run own infra (Tencent/Baidu/ByteDance) or use neoclouds rather than hyperscalers). Editorial assumption.

entries.valueBasis: Q1 2026 annualised — Cursor compute spend on AI-native compute (token factories + neoclouds)
entries.evidence: SemiAnalysis 2025 commentary — Cursor blended COGS ~45% of $1B ARR; majority via direct frontier APIs (not aiNativeCompute), residual ~10% via aiNativeCompute serving
entries.rationale: Cursor mostly hits frontier API endpoints directly (Anthropic, OpenAI). aiNativeCompute exposure modest.

entries.valueBasis: Q1 2026 annualised — Perplexity compute spend on aiNativeCompute (residual, mostly direct frontier API)
entries.evidence: Perplexity press 2025 — most spend through frontier APIs; small share via Together/Fireworks for OSS-model serving
entries.rationale: Most apps in this layer hit frontier APIs directly; aiNativeCompute share is small.

longTailMultiplier.baseValueBasis: Aggregate ai_native_app ARR ex-named entries — see arrModel.apps.aiNative residual
longTailMultiplier.rationale: Smaller AI-Native Apps spend ~8% of ARR on AI-Native Compute (token-factory inference for OSS models, residual after most spend goes direct to frontier APIs or hyperscalers). Editorial.

entries.valueBasis: Q1 2026 annualised — CoreWeave revenue from Microsoft sub-rent for OpenAI workload
entries.evidence: CoreWeave 10-Q customer concentration; ~85% of CoreWeave $6B run-rate is Microsoft sub-rent
entries.rationale: Critical double-count guard. Microsoft's $37B AI run-rate already includes Azure capacity powered by CoreWeave GPUs. CoreWeave's revenue from MSFT must be deducted from the gross-summed compute layer to avoid counting the same dollars twice.

entries.valueBasis: Q1 2026 annualised — Fireworks compute backed by AWS
entries.evidence: Token factory infra mostly on hyperscaler primary; ~50% of Fireworks $140M ARR backs onto AWS GPU instances (editorial)
entries.rationale: Token factories run on hyperscaler GPU capacity; pass-through estimated at ~50% of their ARR.

entries.valueBasis: Q1 2026 annualised — Together compute backed by AWS
entries.evidence: Editorial — same logic as Fireworks (~50% of $130M ARR)

longTailMultiplier.baseValueBasis: Aggregate aiNativeCompute ARR ex-named entries (Lambda, Crusoe, Nebius, Groq, Replicate, Anyscale, Fal.ai)
longTailMultiplier.rationale: Smaller neoclouds and token factories pass ~30% of ARR through to trad-compute (most have own datacenters or rent from hyperscaler partners; Nebius is full owner-operator so contributes 0 to long tail; weighted avg 30%). Editorial.
